behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on a real AI/ML embedding model response. A stubbed embedding client can hide model/deployment mismatches (for example, model version or endpoint returning a different dimension). I considered Workflow, but rejected it because this behavior checks one model-dependent step (embedding dimensionality), not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with representative queries and assert returned vector length equals the index dimension configured in the real search stack.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: The likely failure is module handoff/configuration (query vector -> vector DB query -> score mapping/order) rather than single-function logic. Real OS/network/subprocess is not explicitly required by the behavior text, and correctness does not depend on model output quality. I considered System, but rejected it because the behavior text does not explicitly require real external HTTP/API contract validation; this can be validated with integrated local components.
behaviors.test_description: Using a controlled local test index with known vectors, run the actual search pipeline and assert scores are monotonic descending and aligned with returned order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: This directly tests LLM judgment/steering effect on ranking for nuanced intent. Deterministic mocks cannot validate whether the model actually reorders meaningfully. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the pass condition targets one model-dependent decision point (the reranker output), not multiple agentic decisions across steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run reranking against the real LLM on a curated nuanced-query fixture set and assert at least one rank position changes versus raw similarity order for designated cases.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily thresholding/handoff behavior across search modules (scoring + filtering + response assembly). It does not inherently require real AI output quality or real infrastructure per the behavior text. I considered System, but rejected it because the text does not explicitly require real external contract checks over HTTP; integrated in-process verification can detect the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test index where query vectors are intentionally far from all items, execute the real search and filtering pipeline, and assert zero results are emitted.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end SLO across multiple sequential model-dependent steps (embedding + retrieval + LLM rerank). The median latency outcome depends on the chained pipeline, not a single component. I considered Agentic and System, but rejected both because the behavior explicitly measures multi-step end-to-end performance across more than one agentic stage.
behaviors.test_description: Run representative production-like queries through the full live pipeline (embedding model, vector DB, and LLM reranker), record end-to-end durations over enough samples, and assert median latency is under 2.0 seconds.
